title: Biking and Beach Drawings
summary:
We decided that afternoon to go bike at the Whiskey Run trails outside Coos Bay, Oregon. The trails were some of the most fun flowing mountain bike-specific trails that I've been on. We biked for two hours and had a nice little picnic. Maya was happy that for the first time in her life, she was biking in temperatures less than 70°. After our bike adventure, we met up with Abby at a brewery in Coos Bay to have a beer and some coffee. Coos Bay was a cute little town and we were happy to have taken a pit stop there. After a beer, we went to Fred Meyer's where I spent far too long figuring out how to download their app and get a coupon for two dollars off some granola bars. Luckily I was successful! Our camp spot for the night was the Eel River Campground just outside of town where we spent the night with Abby. She made us a yummy chicken and rice Asian soup that we had for dinner before heading to bed a little early. The next morning we hiked up to the top of a sand dune right outside camp and did our best impressions of the sand shuffle from the movie Dune. After a cup of coffee, we hit the road towards Eugene.

-Colin

photos.caption: A selfie during our bike ride at the whiskey run trails.
